Title: Efficient assembly of long DNA fragments and multiple genes with improved nickase-based cloning and Cre/loxP recombination

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies essential intermediate for target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Red pigment reporter in engineered tissues
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves; rice calli; leaves and roots of transgenic rice plants
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for betanin biosynthesis; with BvDODA1S and cDOPA5GT enabled betanin accumulation, and without full cassette only yellow pigment/other betalains accumulated
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BvCYP76AD1S was one of the core engineered pathway genes used to reconstruct betanin biosynthesis in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves and rice. 2. Co-expression with BvDODA1S and cDOPA5GT was necessary for betanin production. 3. Inclusion in the four-gene cassette with ADH further increased betanin accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Multigene transient expression in N. benthamiana; stable transformation in rice calli/plants; qRT-PCR; pigment phenotype observation; betanin quantification
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in infiltrated N. benthamiana leaves and red transgenic rice plants when introduced as transgene
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Agrobacterium-mediated transient leaf infiltration; stable rice transformation under hygromycin selection
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overaccumulation of betanin in calli may negatively affect plant regeneration; repeated use of P35S promoter may cause instability/loss of transgenes
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as a pathway module for visible metabolic engineering and reporter-assisted multigene stacking; should be stacked with BvDODA1S and cDOPA5GT, optionally ADH, while limiting repeated promoter use
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study used codon-optimized sequences; exact enzyme step assignment was not dissected experimentally here.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms key precursor of target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Red pigment reporter in engineered tissues
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves; rice calli; leaves and roots of transgenic rice plants
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for betanin formation together with BvCYP76AD1S and cDOPA5GT
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BvDODA1S was essential in the engineered betanin pathway. 2. Three-gene combinations containing BvDODA1S, BvCYP76AD1S and cDOPA5GT produced betanin. 3. Additional ADH further enhanced betanin accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Multigene transient expression in N. benthamiana; stable transformation in rice; qRT-PCR; pigment phenotype observation; betanin quantification
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in infiltrated N. benthamiana leaves and red transgenic rice plants when introduced as transgene
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Agrobacterium-mediated transient expression and stable transformation
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Same construct-level tradeoffs as other betanin genes: repeated promoter use may destabilize transgenes; high betanin may impair regeneration
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Core biosynthetic gene for engineering visible betalain pigmentation in plants
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as a codon-optimized transgene; exact accession not given in main text.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate precursor contributing directly to target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Red pigment reporter in engineered tissues
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves; rice calli; leaves and roots of transgenic rice plants
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Necessary with BvCYP76AD1S and BvDODA1S for betanin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. cDOPA5GT was a required engineered enzyme in the reconstructed betanin pathway. 2. Co-expression with BvCYP76AD1S and BvDODA1S enabled betanin accumulation. 3. Addition of ADH increased final pigment levels.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression in N. benthamiana; stable rice transformation; qRT-PCR; pigment visualization; betanin assay
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in infiltrated leaves and red rice transformants
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Agrobacterium-mediated transient expression and stable transformation
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Construct instability with excessive repeated P35S use; high betanin may hinder regeneration
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential glycosylation module for plant betanin engineering
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Source species was not explicitly stated in the provided text.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Acts as an auxiliary enzyme in the engineered betanin module; the paper shows it enhances betanin accumulation when added to the three-gene cassette
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Red pigment reporter in engineered tissues
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves; rice calli; leaves and roots of transgenic rice plants
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Addition of ADH further promoted betanin biosynthesis beyond the three-gene cassette
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ADH was not strictly required for initial betanin synthesis but enhanced pigment accumulation. 2. Four-gene CDGA cassettes produced darker red coloration than three-gene CDG cassettes. 3. The enhancement was validated in both tobacco leaves and rice.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative multigene cassette expression (CDG vs CDGA); transient expression; stable transformation; qRT-PCR; pigment phenotype; betanin quantification
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in infiltrated N. benthamiana leaves and red transgenic rice plants when present
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Same construct-level issues: repeated P35S promoter reuse and possible regeneration penalty from excess betanin
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful booster gene to enhance betanin output in engineered pathways
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The exact metabolic role of ADH in this system was not mechanistically resolved in this paper.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to immunity and cell death in rice
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Included as a pathway gene in the stacked HP cluster for hydroxycinnamoylputrescine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsODC was one of three rice HP-cluster genes assembled into donor vectors. 2. It contributes precursor supply for hydroxycinnamoylputrescine biosynthesis. 3. The study used it mainly to demonstrate multigene stacking capacity rather than phenotyping metabolite output here.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: UNiEDA cloning; construct verification by colony PCR and PmeI/SbfI digestion; Cre/loxP stacking
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Used with its native promoter and terminator in stacked construct
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be combined with OsPHT genes to engineer phenolamide-related defense metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No direct metabolite assay or transgenic phenotyping of the HP pathway was provided in this paper.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Putrescine hydroxycinnamoyl acyltransferase; BAHD acyltransferase family
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers hydroxycinnamoyl group to putrescine to produce hydroxycinnamoylputrescine
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to immunity and cell death in rice
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Part of the HP biosynthetic gene cluster stacked into the vector system
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsPHT3 was included as one of the hydroxycinnamoylputrescine pathway genes. 2. It was assembled successfully into donor and acceptor constructs. 3. Its pathway role was cited as contributing to disease resistance-related phenolamide metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: UNiEDA cloning and digestion-based construct validation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Used with its native promoter and terminator
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for stacking to enhance defense-associated phenolamide metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No direct functional assay in this article beyond pathway inclusion and construct assembly.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Putrescine hydroxycinnamoyl acyltransferase; BAHD acyltransferase family
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers hydroxycinnamoyl moieties to putrescine in hydroxycinnamoylputrescine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to immunity and cell death in rice
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Part of HP pathway cluster assembled for multigene stacking demonstration
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsPHT4 was stacked with OsODC and OsPHT3 into donor vectors. 2. It belongs to the hydroxycinnamoylputrescine pathway associated with rice immunity and cell death. 3. This paper mainly validates assembly rather than metabolite effect.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: UNiEDA cloning and digestion-based construct verification
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Used with native promoter and terminator
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful in multigene defense-metabolism engineering stacks
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No direct metabolite assay in this study.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes ATP-dependent condensation of glutamate and ammonium to form glutamine
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Included in stacked GS module for nitrogen assimilation engineering
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsGS1;1 was one of three glutamine synthetase genes assembled into donor vectors. 2. It represents a nitrogen assimilation pathway gene used to test multigene assembly. 3. No direct metabolic phenotyping was performed in this paper.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: UNiEDA cloning; digestion verification; Cre/loxP stacking
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Used with native promoter and terminator
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for nitrogen-use-efficiency multigene engineering when stacked with other GS genes
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study cites known GS function but does not test glutamine levels here.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes glutamine formation from glutamate and ammonium in nitrogen assimilation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Included as one of the stacked GS genes
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsGS1;2 was assembled into donor vectors as part of a three-GS gene module. 2. It was used to demonstrate capacity for stacking nutrient-metabolism genes. 3. No direct glutamine phenotype was measured here.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: UNiEDA cloning; digestion verification; Cre/loxP stacking
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Used with native promoter and terminator
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential component of multigene nitrogen assimilation engineering stacks
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No direct functional assay in this paper.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes ATP-dependent synthesis of glutamine from glutamate and ammonium
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Included in the stacked GS cassette for demonstrating high-capacity assembly
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsGS1;3 was part of the three-gene GS stack. 2. The paper cites its role in nitrogen assimilation and recycling. 3. Here it served mainly as a functional cargo gene for multigene assembly.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: UNiEDA cloning; digestion verification; Cre/loxP stacking
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Used with native promoter and terminator
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential component for engineering glutamine biosynthesis and nitrogen-use traits
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No direct measurement of glutamine accumulation in this study.
